name: Quick Feet
source: Chris Atkinson · Y3–Y4 long jump session card

ks1.objective: Rehearse fast, light ground contacts on the spot.

lks2.objective: Rehearse the SPEED of ground contact — the 'quick' in 'quick strong push'. Light contacts, tall body.

uks2.objective: Sustained fast ground contacts under control — quality not just quantity.
successCriteria: Contacts stay light at high tempo
Arms drive in opposition to legs

equipment.item: Open space — one body-width per child

space.description: Open space, indoor or outdoor. Each child needs a body-width of clear space. No equipment needed — bodies only.

steps: All children stand in their own space, feet under hips.
Coach: "The ground is HOT — like hot sand. Don't let your feet stay down."
On "GO", they run on the spot — light, fast contacts. Touch and lift.
Body stays TALL. Arms join in, mirroring the rhythm.
Coach calls tempo: "QUICK quick quick QUICK!" — voice carries the speed.
Coach calls "STOP" after 30 seconds. Pause. Reset.
Optional: one more round, slightly longer.

cues.trigger: if they get heavy or slumped

errors.fix: "Light, not hard — like hot sand!" Demo a heavy stomp vs a light tap. Make it physical.

errors.cause: Trying too hard to move quickly
errors.fix: "TALL — eyes UP!" Speed comes from the legs, not the body lean.

errors.issue: Arms by the sides, doing nothing
errors.fix: Coach demo: arms join in, opposition to legs. "Arms mirror your feet."

progressions: Add count: "how many touches in 10 seconds?" — count by tens.
Add claps every 5th touch — adds a rhythm/coordination layer.
Travel version: forward 2 m of fast feet (don't break form).
Strong: combine with a STOP cue and freeze in tall position.
questions: What does QUICK push feel like in your FEET?
What's the OPPOSITE of heavy feet?
When in your jump will you use this kind of quick push?
base.showmanship: Coach's voice carries the tempo — "QUICK quick quick QUICK!" Vocal energy multiplies physical energy. Bring it big.
